title: Recommendation Human Review Set
purpose: Small repeatable MVP review set for recommendation quality changes. These cases are review fixtures, not product behavior.

reviewScale.pass: Top results satisfy the explicit primary intent and no severe mismatch is hidden.
reviewScale.mixed: At least one important constraint is met, but a follow-up fix is worth tracking.
reviewScale.fail: Top results violate the primary intent or weak-result visibility is missing.

cases.scenario: Workspace request where the user explicitly asks for a chair under a low budget.

input.requestText: 밝고 미니멀한 작업 공간에 저렴한 의자를 두고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: Top 3 should include at least one clear chair or chair-like product.
A non-chair can be acceptable only if it is a plausible workspace support item.
If chair coverage is weak, the response should expose weak-category signals.

cases.scenario: Low-budget living-room request with a sofa preference.

input.requestText: 예산이 낮아서 가성비 위주로 소파 중심 거실을 맞추고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: Budget fit should not be sacrificed to force a sofa when catalog coverage is weak.
If no sofa is available in the top results, weak-category surfacing is acceptable and expected.
Avoid premium-looking top results unless explicitly marked as weak budget fit.

cases.scenario: Warm wood and calm style request with a table preference.

input.requestText: 따뜻한 우드톤과 차분한 느낌으로 작은 테이블을 맞추고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: At least one top result should have explicit or proxy warm/calm style fit.
Wood, brown, natural, grey, beige, or similarly calm signals should be treated as positive.
A visually harsh modern item should not dominate unless other constraints clearly justify it.

cases.scenario: Dining room request where table fit matters more than generic visual similarity.

input.requestText: 모던한 다이닝 공간에 작은 테이블을 두고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: Top results should include dining-appropriate table or chair support.
A sofa-led result is a failure for this case.
Modern style can be explicit text fit or proxy fit, but room fit must remain visible.

cases.scenario: Mixed category, style, budget, and room-type request for a workspace chair.

input.requestText: 밝고 미니멀한 작업 공간으로 가볍게 꾸미고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: This is the core regression case for the current MVP ranking baseline.
A top result can miss one secondary signal only if the rest of the top 3 still satisfies the intent.
The result should remain deduped and purchasable-looking.

cases.scenario: Intentionally awkward request for a low-budget sofa in a small workspace.

input.requestText: 작은 작업 공간인데 저예산으로 소파를 꼭 넣고 싶어요

humanReviewFocus: This case is allowed to be weak because the catalog may not satisfy sofa + workspace + low budget.
The important pass condition is honest weak-result surfacing, not forcing a bad sofa.
If the API returns confident-looking non-sofa results without weak-category visibility, mark fail.
